volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 1.2.135 - Section 2.1.230)

sections.label: Section 1.2.135
sections.original: Hic meus amicus : emit eam dono mihi ,
sections.translation: this friend of mine was present ; he bought her as a gift to me , not knowing or suspecting any thing of all this . He returned ; but when he perceived that I had formed a connection with you as well , he feigned excuses on purpose that he might not give her ; he said that if he could feel confidence that he should be preferred to yourself

sections.label: Section 1.2.140
sections.original: Apud me , ac non id metuat , ne ubi acceperim
sections.translation: by me , so as not to apprehend that , when I had received her , I should forsake him , then he was ready to give her to me ; but that he did fear this . But , so far as I can conjecture , he has set his affections upon the girl .

sections.label: Section 1.2.143b
sections.translation: Any thing beyond that ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.144
sections.original: Nihil ; nam quaesivi . Nunc ego eam , mi Phaedria ,
sections.translation: Nothing ; for I have made inquiry . Now , my Phaedria ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.145
sections.original: Multae sunt causae quamobrem cupiam abducere :
sections.translation: there are many reasons why I could wish to get her away from him . In the first place , because she was called my sister ; moreover , that I may restore and deliver her to her friends . I am a lone woman ; I have no one here , neither acquaintance nor relative ; wherefore , Phaedria , I am desirous by my good offices to secure friends .

sections.label: Section 1.2.150
sections.original: Id amabo adiuta me , quo id fiat facilius .
sections.translation: Prithee , do aid me in this , in order that it may be the more easily effected . Do allow him for the few next days to have the preference with me . Do you make no answer ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.152b
sections.translation: Most vile woman ! Can I make you any answer after such behavior as this ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.154
sections.original: Eu noster ! laudo : tandem perdoluit : vir es .
sections.translation: Well done , my master , I commend you ; ( aside ) he ’ s galled at last . ( To PHAEDRIA .) You show yourself a man .

sections.label: Section 1.2.155
sections.original: At ego nescibam quorsum tu ires : Parvula
sections.translation: I was not aware what you were aiming at ; she was carried away from here , when a little child ; my mother brought her up as though her own ; she was called my sister ; I wish to get her away , that I may restore her to her friends . The meaning is , that all these expressions , in fine , now amount to this , that I am shut out , he is admitted . For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.160
sections.original: Nisi illum plus amas quam me , et istam nunc times
sections.translation: Except that you love him more than me : and now you are afraid of her who has been brought hither , lest she should win him , such as he is , from yourself .

sections.label: Section 1.2.162
sections.translation: I , afraid of that ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.162b
sections.original: Quid te ergo aliud sollicitat ? cedo .
sections.translation: What else , then , gives you concern ? Let me know . Is he the only person who makes presents ? Have you found my bounty shut against you ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.165
sections.original: Nonne ubi mihi dixti cupere te ex Aethiopia
sections.translation: Did I not , when you told me that you wished for a servant-maid from Aethiopia , [*] setting all other matters aside , go and seek for one ? Then you said that you wanted a Eunuch , because ladies of quality [*] alone make use of them ; I found you one . I yesterday paid twenty minae [*] for them both .

sections.label: Section 1.2.170
sections.original: Tamen contemptus abs te haec habui in memoria :
sections.translation: Though slighted by you , I still kept these things in mind ; as a reward for so doing , I am despised by you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.171b
sections.translation: Phaedria , what does this mean ? Although I wish to get her away , and think that by these means it could most probably be effected ; still , rather than make an enemy of you , I ’ ll do as you request me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: Utinam istuc verbum ex animo ac vere diceres ,
sections.translation: I only wish that you used that expression from your heart and truthfully , rather than make an enemy of you . If I could believe that this was said sincerely , I could put up with any thing .

sections.label: Section 1.2.178
sections.original: Labascit victus uno verbo quam cito !
sections.translation: He staggers ; how instantaneously is he vanquished by a single expression !

sections.label: Section 1.2.179
sections.original: Ego non ex animo misera dico ? quam ioco
sections.translation: I , wretched woman , not speak from my heart ? What , pray ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.180
sections.original: Rem voluisti a me tandem quin perfeceris ?
sections.translation: did you ever ask of me in jest , but that you carried your point ? I am unable to obtain even this of you , that you would grant me only two days .

sections.label: Section 1.2.182b
sections.translation: If , indeed , it is but two days ; but don ’ t let these days become twenty .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184
sections.translation: Assuredly not more than two days , or —

sections.label: Section 1.2.184b
sections.translation: Or ? I won ’ t have it .

sections.label: Section 1.2.185
sections.original: Non fiet : hoc modo sine te exorem .
sections.translation: It shall not be ; only do allow me to obtain this of you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.185b
sections.translation: Of course that which you desire must be done .

sections.label: Section 1.2.186b
sections.translation: I love you as you deserve ; you act obligingly .

sections.label: Section 1.2.186c
sections.translation: I shall go into the country ; there I shall worry myself for the next two days : I ’ m resolved to do so ; Thais must be humored . Do you , Parmeno , take care that they are brought hither .

sections.label: Section 1.2.189b
sections.translation: Certainly .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190
sections.translation: For the next two days then , Thais , adieu .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190b
sections.translation: And the same to you , my Phaedria ; do you desire aught else ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.191b
sections.translation: What should I desire ? That , present with the Captain , you may be as if absent ; that night and day you may love me ; may feel my absence ; may dream of me ; may be impatient for me ; may think about me ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.195
sections.original: Me speres ; me te oblectes ; mecum tota sis :
sections.translation: may hope for me ; may centre your delight in me ; may be all in all with me ; in fine , if you will , be my very life , as I am yours .

sections.label: Section 1.2.197
sections.original: Me miseram ! forsitan hic mihi parvam habeat fidem ,
sections.translation: Ah wretched me ! [*] perhaps now he puts but little faith in me , and forms his estimate of me from the dispositions of other women . [*] By my troth , I , who know my own self , am very sure of this ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.200
sections.original: Neque me finxisse falsi quicquam , neque meo
sections.translation: that I have not feigned any thing that ’ s false , and that no person is dearer to my heart than this same Phaedria ; and whatever in the present case I have done , for this girl ’ s sake have I done it ; for I trust that now I have pretty nearly discovered her brother , a young man of very good family ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.205
sections.original: Et is hodie venturum ad me constituit domum .
sections.translation: and he has appointed this day to come to me at my house . I ’ ll go hence in-doors , and wait until he comes .

sections.label: Section 2.1.207
sections.original: Fac ita ut iussi deducantur isti .
sections.translation: Mind that those people are taken there , as I ordered .

sections.label: Section 2.1.207b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 2.1.207c
sections.translation: And carefully .

sections.label: Section 2.1.208
sections.translation: It shall be done .

sections.label: Section 2.1.208b
sections.translation: And with all speed .

sections.label: Section 2.1.208c
sections.translation: It shall be done .

sections.label: Section 2.1.208d
sections.translation: Have you had sufficient instructions ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.208e
sections.translation: Dear me ! to ask the question , as though it were a matter of difficulty .

sections.label: Section 2.1.210
sections.original: Utinam tam aliquid invenire facile possis Phaedria
sections.translation: I wish that you were able , Phaedria , to find any thing as easily as this present will be lost .

sections.label: Section 2.1.211b
sections.original: Ego quoque una pereo , quod mihi est carius :
sections.translation: Together with it , I myself am lost , which concerns me more nearly . Don ’ t bear this with such a feeling of vexation .

sections.label: Section 2.1.212b
sections.translation: By no means ; on the contrary , I ’ ll see it done . But do you order any thing else ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.214
sections.original: Munus nostrum ornato verbis , quod poteris ; et istum aemulum ,
sections.translation: Set off my present with words , as far as you can ; and so far as you are able , do drive away that rival of mine from her .

sections.label: Section 2.1.216
sections.translation: Pshaw ! I should have kept that in mind , even if you hadn ’ t reminded me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.216b
sections.original: Ego rus ibo , atque ibi manebo .
sections.translation: I shall go into the country and remain there .

sections.label: Section 2.1.217
sections.translation: I agree with you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.217b
sections.translation: But hark you !

sections.label: Section 2.1.217c
sections.translation: What is it you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.217d
sections.translation: Are you of opinion that I can muster resolution and hold out so as not to come back within the time ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.218b
sections.translation: What , you ? Upon my faith , I don ’ t think so ; for either you ’ ll be returning at once , or by-and-by , at night , want of sleep will be driving you hither .

sections.label: Section 2.1.220
sections.original: Opus faciam ut defatiger usque ingratiis ut dormiam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll do some laborious work , that I may be continually fatigued , so as to sleep in spite of myself .

sections.label: Section 2.1.221
sections.translation: When wearied , you will be keeping awake ; by this you will be making it worse .

sections.label: Section 2.1.221b
sections.translation: Oh , you talk to no purpose , Parmeno : this softness of spirit , upon my faith , must be got rid of ; I indulge myself too much . Could I not do without her , pray , if there were the necessity , even for a whole three days ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.223b
sections.translation: Whew ! an entire three days ! Take care what you are about .

sections.label: Section 2.1.224b
sections.translation: My mind is made up .

sections.label: Section 2.1.225
sections.original: Di boni , quid hoc morbi est ? adeone homines immutarier
sections.translation: Good Gods ! What a malady is this ! That a man should become so changed through love , that you wouldn ’ t know him to be the same person ! Not any one was there [*] less inclined to folly than he , and no one more discreet or more temperate . But who is it that ’ s coming this way ? Heyday ! surely this is Gnatho , the Captain ’ s Parasite ; he ’ s bringing along with him the damsel as a present to her . Heavens !

sections.label: Section 2.1.230
sections.original: Facie honesta : mirum ni ego me turpiter hodie hic dabo
sections.translation: How beautiful ! No wonder if I make but a sorry figure here to-day with this decrepit Eunuch of mine . She surpasses Thais herself .
